fault_hypothesis: On line 61, the condition `adjusted_now > ttl` uses strict greater-than, so when `now == ttl` (and clock-skew tolerance is 0), the entry is incorrectly considered valid. The condition should be `adjusted_now >= ttl` to expire entries when now equals the TTL.

spec.expr: not (adjusted_now__1 == ttl__1 and __cbfl_result is not None)

constraints.intent: When adjusted_now equals ttl, the function should return None (expired), not the payload.
After computing adjusted_now, if it is >= ttl then the entry must be considered expired (the check on line 61 uses > but should use >=).
Placeholder sanity check: ttl__1 is accessible before the comparison on line 61.
In the else-branch (entry not expired), adjusted_now must be strictly less than ttl; equality should have triggered expiry.
